2026-04-17.thesis: Iran threatens Persian Gulf/Hormuz naval blockade; no deal signed
2026-04-17.exit_case: Iran is actively talking to the US — a diplomatic deal could resolve the threat within days.
2026-04-17.assessment: Week 1. Catalyst is fresh and live: Iran has issued an explicit, named threat to close the Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, and Red Sea to all traffic unless Washington relents — the single most consequential chokepoint risk globally (~20% of seaborne oil). No deal has been signed; talks are ongoing but inconclusive. Tanker day-rates are already elevated and BWET is extending its advance, confirming the market is pricing real disruption risk. Coverage remains niche (RNZ, etf.com) — no mainstream saturation, no retail frenzy, no sell-side target-raising circus yet. BWET is confirmed purest vehicle as a freight-futures ETN with no dilution from tanker-operator equity risk. The open diplomatic channel is the primary risk — a surprise deal announcement would instantly collapse this thesis — but until ink is dry, the threat is the catalyst, and the catalyst is live. Hold.
2026-04-17.exit_advice: Exit when Iran formally signs a nuclear/sanctions deal with the US, explicitly withdraws the blockade threat, or tanker day-rates collapse signalling market-perceived risk removal.
